input: Explain how vector embeddings work and why they're useful for semantic search. Explain it so a non-technical founder can understand it.
criteria: uses an analogy or real-world comparison (not just abstract math)
explains what a vector is without using the word 'vector' as the definition
explains why similar concepts end up 'near' each other in the embedding space
explains the practical benefit for search (find things even without exact keyword match)
does not use unexplained jargon (cosine similarity, dimensionality, etc. must be explained if used)
can be understood by someone with no ML background
judge_prompt: You are evaluating an explanation written by an AI research assistant for a non-technical audience. Score 0-10. Full marks: all criteria met, the explanation would genuinely help a non-technical founder understand and make a decision about using vector search. Deduct 2 points for any unexplained jargon. Deduct 2 if no analogy is used. Deduct 2 if the practical business benefit is not clearly stated.
